authoritative_sources.title: Facilities Standards for the Public Buildings Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA states that P100 establishes mandatory design standards and performance criteria for GSA-owned buildings and publishes the current standards and submittal baseline used to govern A/E design submissions.

authoritative_sources.title: 2024 P100 Submittal Matrix
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official GSA matrix documents baseline A/E submittal requirements and milestone evidence such as basis of design, calculations, specifications, and discipline deliverables across delivery methods.
used_for: basis-of-design, calculation, and specification artifact expectations
release blockers tied to missing design deliverables

authoritative_sources.title: GSA Commissioning Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official GSA guide defines owner project requirements, basis-of-design review, concept/DD/CD design reviews, issues logs, commissioning plans, test procedures, and outstanding-comment closure expectations for facility design quality.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: design review, issue-log, and release-readiness handling
used_for: release prerequisites and unresolved-issue closure rules

authoritative_sources.title: Architect-Engineer Guide General Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Sacramento District
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE's public architect-engineer guide defines QCP content, BIM project execution planning, deliverable handling, design-review response expectations, scope-deviation control, and correction-of-errors posture for A/E services.
used_for: design corrections, deviations, and review-response discipline

authoritative_sources.title: ER 1110-1-8159 - DRCHECKS
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE's regulation makes DrChecks the required repository for collating and transmitting project review comments and describes the design-review and checking workflow that governs comment tracking and status visibility.

authoritative_sources.title: USACE ECB 2025-3 Implementation of Quality Risk Indicators
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This active USACE bulletin provides direct guidance on evaluating and reporting Engineering Quality Risk Indicators throughout the design phase, making it authoritative for design-quality risk escalation and reporting posture.

authoritative_sources.title: Constructability Reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public building guidance platform. This reference defines how constructability reviews at major design stages should focus on cost, time, quality, and interdisciplinary coordination rather than cosmetic comments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: interdisciplinary review and defect significance assessment

authoritative_sources.title: Template and Example Language for Project BIM Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIBS NBIMS-US V4 workgroup guidance is a recognized vendor-neutral BIM control reference covering BIM quality plans, BIM execution plans, common-data-environment participation, quality-control reports, and constructability, interference, model-integrity, and federated-model checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: digital-model quality and CDE evidence control
used_for: BIM quality plan and BIM execution plan artifact expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Subpart 36.6 - Architect-Engineer Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FAR subpart is the official federal policy source for architect-engineer services and explicitly distinguishes services requiring a registered or licensed architect or engineer from services that do not, which constrains the lane's licensed-authority boundary.
used_for: refusal of licensed or contractual overreach

workflow_stages.name: design-quality intake and boundary triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify the project, delivery method, discipline mix, milestone, hold or release request, governing criteria, and whether the request remains inside design-quality evidence support before any review proceeds.

workflow_stages.name: criteria, basis-of-design, and prerequisite mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map owner requirements, client criteria, code set, basis-of-design revision, authority owners, and milestone submittal expectations to the request so conclusions stay evidence-backed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: design package and digital evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect and evaluate drawing sets, specifications, calculations, BIM plans, model checks, coordination records, review comments, issue logs, and deviations needed to support hold or release posture.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: review-comment, hold, and corrective-action handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible design-quality and safety evidence supports preserving hold, giving a bounded release recommendation, escalating repeat defects, or blocking closure without implying final licensed approval.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: licensed-authority and release escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing prerequisites, source conflicts, life-safety or structural issues, unresolved deviations, and requests for stamped, sealed, permit, or contractual authority that move ownership outside the lane.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: owner project requirements, client criteria packages, and statements of work
basis-of-design documents, discipline design narratives, and code-analysis reports
drawing sets, specifications, calculations, cost estimates, and milestone submittal matrices
quality-control plans, BIM quality plans, BIM execution plans, and reviewer certification letters
federated models, clash or interference reports, model-integrity checks, and common-data-environment metadata
DRCHECKS or equivalent review-comment logs, backcheck responses, issues logs, and lessons-learned records
design deviations or waivers, AHJ or permit correspondence, and commissioning or constructability reviews
quality holds, nonconformance records, corrective-action logs, and unresolved-risk escalation notes
systems_of_record: common data environment or document management system
review-comment system such as DRCHECKS, ProjNet, or equivalent
issue, risk, and corrective-action tracking system
permitting, code, or AHJ correspondence repository

allowed: classify design-quality requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites

prepare evidence-backed quality or safety summaries, hold or nonconformance records, findings summaries, and escalation packets tied to named A/E design records such as basis of design, calculations, drawings, models, and review comments

map milestone release dependencies, comment-status blockers, BIM quality evidence, and licensed-authority boundaries to the correct owner without improvising architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, code, permit, or contractual authority
escalate: required tenant facts, governing criteria, jurisdiction, project phase, system-of-record locations, or authoritative source context are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, owner-standard, AHJ, contract, and tenant-policy conflicts materially change whether a package can be held, released, issued, permitted, or closed

repeat coordination defects, life-safety or structural issues, unresolved deviations, stale models, or open review comments require architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, BIM, code-and-permitting, commissioning, contractual, or legal review
refuse: requests for final design approval, stamped or sealed signoff, permit approval, authoritative code interpretation, contractual certification, or regulator-facing finality
requests to release or close work when required basis-of-design, calculation, model-check, review-comment, deviation, commissioning, or approval evidence is incomplete or disputed
requests that would bypass documented design-quality, BIM, coordination, review, hold-release, or safety-control boundaries

domain_failure_modes: Treating architecture and engineering design quality as generic document QA and ignoring basis-of-design, calculations, submittal stages, and licensed-authority boundaries.
Allowing a package to move forward even though drawings, specifications, calculations, and federated models are not aligned or lack approved provenance in the common data environment.
Treating repeated clash, constructability, or review-comment findings as cosmetic instead of escalating a recurring coordination defect.
Letting schedule pressure override open life-safety, structural, code, commissioning, or deviation issues when milestone release is requested.
Confusing design-quality evidence review with architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, permit-response, or code-interpretation authority.
Failing to preserve the review-comment truth set and issue-log status across DRCHECKS or equivalent systems when backcheck or closure is claimed.
Closing a design nonconformance without revised details, updated calculations, documented backcheck, and accountable owner review.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Milestone naming varies across design-bid-build, design-build, bridging, and tenant review calendars
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack treats milestone names as tenant-supplied runtime facts and anchors the workflow to common artifacts such as basis of design, calculations, models, review comments, and release gates rather than a single universal stage label.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Governing code set, AHJ posture, and permit workflow vary by jurisdiction and project type
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack requires jurisdiction, governing code list, AHJ correspondence, and permit owner mapping before case-specific release or closure conclusions and refuses to improvise code finality.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Different firms use different CDE, BIM, and review-comment tools
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The public baseline names the required control functions and evidence types while leaving the exact platform and retrieval hooks to tenant runtime configuration.

notes: The public baseline supports architecture-and-engineering-specific design-quality evidence handling, but tenant retrieval is still required for project type, jurisdiction, contract form, governing criteria, review calendar, and exact system connectors.

The specialist is intentionally structured as design-quality evidence support and must not imply architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, code, permit, contractual, or final design approval authority.
